title: Record bounded archive-growth budget for release reproducibility follow-up
rawUserRequest: 继续完成当前文档工单；为发布归档修复产生的不可变生命周期证据记录建立有界、可审计的复杂度预算，并完成完整 PR、合并、清理和默认分支同步流程。

declaredIntent.summary: 将新增的一个不可变 Work Item 归档记录纳入现有复杂度预算，并记录偿还责任。

outOfScope: Changing release archive bytes or publishing a tag.
Deleting or rewriting existing immutable Work Item archives.
Starting any plan implementation Work Item.

sources.reason: Authoritative complexity ceilings and repayment ledger.
Evidence for the additional immutable archive record.
Cross-platform regression expectations for the confirmed ceiling.

riskAssessment.reason: Only the confirmed archive-growth ceiling and its repayment record change; existing archives remain immutable.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized completion of the full release plan and its lifecycle closure.

preReviewWarnings: The budget must explain the measured growth and preserve a later repayment owner.
acceptance: Complexity policy accepts the measured archive sequence without weakening existing guards.
Repayment ownership and reason are recorded in the policy ledger.
Finish Gate, PR checks, merge, closure, and default-branch synchronization complete.
guidelines: Do not rewrite or delete immutable Work Item archives.
Governance budget changes must be explicit, bounded, and repayable.

intent.problem: The release archive reproducibility follow-up adds one required immutable lifecycle archive, raising archiveGrowth from 470 to 471 and causing the confirmed ceiling check to fail.
constraints: Do not publish before the full Work Item lifecycle closes.
intent.rationale: A separate governance Work Item keeps the release fix and budget calibration independent while making the new evidence cost explicit.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The repository records archiveGrowth=471 and passes the complexity guard with a documented repayment entry.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized completion of the full release plan and publication.

rollbackNote: Revert this budget calibration while preserving the immutable archive evidence.
